purpose: Organizing macro scenarios for dollar direction: risk-off strength (left), weak-growth weakness (middle), strong-growth strength (right).
components: Left tail: risk-off dollar strength (potentially impaired by fiscal dominance)
Middle zone: weak-growth dollar weakness (potentially supported by fiscal expansion / asymmetric smirk)
Right tail: strong-growth dollar strength (potentially capped by growth convergence)
Recalibrated range: DXY 95-108 (critiqued as not evidence-derived)
current_assessment: Descriptively useful but analytically limited. Unfalsifiable as typically deployed because three regimes exhaust all macro states. Best treated as scenario-organizing heuristic requiring ex-ante regime identification. Historical counterexamples (2017-18, Q2-Q3 2020) demonstrate predictive failure. Fiscal dollar smile concept provides novel recalibration but awaits testable prediction specification.
limitations: No real-time regime identification rule with documented hit rates produced by any agent
Three regimes exhaustively cover all macro states, making the framework unfalsifiable
Historical counterexamples demonstrate predictive failure in ~20% of year-periods
Recalibrations (e.g., DXY 95-108) not derived from the analytical framework itself
Cannot handle fiscal dominance left-tail bifurcation without ad hoc modification

purpose: Early-warning system for EM currency crises using a five-signal scoring methodology.
components: Signal scoring: 5 signals (specific signals not enumerated in synthesis)
Crisis probability: 80% at 4-5 signals, 45% at 3 signals, 10% at 0-2 signals
False positive rate: 25-30% (typically from preemptive policy adjustment)
Regime type as severity determinant: managed/pegged produce 30-60% discrete devaluations vs continuous adjustment in credible floaters
Current state: 8-12 economies exhibiting 3+ signals
current_assessment: Validated across 15+ episodes since 1980. More actionable and better validated than alternatives per pair_0 debate. Stronger for detection than timing: knowing 3+ signals present narrows crisis window only to within 24 months. Currently 8-12 economies at elevated risk.
limitations: Timing precision limited to within 24 months window
25-30% false positive rate from preemptive adjustment
Specific 5 signals not fully enumerated in available synthesis
Cannot distinguish which of 8-12 elevated-risk economies will actually experience crisis
Framework may cause its own failure if preemptive adjustment prevents crisis

purpose: Quantifying how interest rate differentials drive FX movements conditional on the volatility regime.
components: Low-vol regime: 8-12% R-squared between rate differentials and FX
High-vol regime: 0-2% R-squared
Structural break confirmed by Chow test F=7.2
Maps to Dollar Smile middle zone (low-vol) vs left/right tails (high-vol)
Carry-to-vol deception: nominal carry overstates risk-adjusted return in low-vol
current_assessment: Most empirically robust finding inherited from prior iterations. Load-bearing across all agents frameworks. Practical utility limited by inability to identify vol regime in advance. Valid qualification but not a refutation of empirical finding.
limitations: Vol regime identification in advance is hard, limiting practical utility
Possible circularity with Dollar Smile mapping
R-squared ranges themselves may be regime-variant over longer horizons
Does not specify transition dynamics between vol regimes

purpose: Predicting the transmission chain of a carry trade unwind through asset classes.
components: Stage 1: FX volatility spike (carry currency depreciation)
Stage 2: Rates market disruption (rate differentials compress)
Stage 3: Equity correlation increase (cross-asset contagion)
Stage 4: Credit spread widening (final stage, severity-determining)
Credit cycle position as primary severity discriminant
current_assessment: Inherited from iter_0009 at 6.5/10 confidence. August 2024 episode validated stages 1-3 but credit stage was contained (spreads below danger thresholds). Current credit spreads (IG ~110bp, HY ~380bp) favor contained scenario but 2025-2027 maturity wall introduces time-varying risk. Maturity-dependent correlation bifurcation (2Y negative vs 30Y slightly positive during unwind) is a post-2022 phenomenon that could cause back-end duration hedges to fail.
limitations: Based on limited sample of full-cycle unwinds
August 2024 episode may not be representative of future unwinds
Credit stage severity depends on cycle position which is itself hard to assess
Post-2022 maturity-dependent correlation shift may be transient
No specification of timing between stages

purpose: Estimating fundamental exchange rate fair value by adjusting for relative productivity and terms of trade, distinguishing from simpler PPP measures.
components: Trade-weighted BEER estimate: dollar 8-15% overvalued
PPP comparison: 15-20% vs EUR, 25-35% vs JPY, 5-10% vs GBP
Productivity and terms-of-trade adjustments
1-5 year out-of-sample predictive power (Cheung et al. 2005)
current_assessment: The only quantified fair value estimation across all agents. Directionally useful at 1-5 year horizons but cannot serve as a timing signal due to exorbitant privilege sustaining overvaluation for extended periods.
limitations: Exorbitant privilege can sustain overvaluation for years beyond model horizon
Zero short-term predictive power
AI capex productivity shifts may not be captured in historical calibration
Construction methodology varies across providers

purpose: Decomposing the gap between financial conditions and the policy rate to identify which channels are doing the easing work.
components: Equity channel: ~30-35bp of loosening
Credit spread channel: ~20-25bp of loosening
Mortgage rate channel: ~15-20bp of loosening
FX channel: ~10-15bp of loosening
Actual rate cuts: ~15-20bp of loosening
Total divergence: ~75-100bp easier than 4.25-4.50% policy rate
current_assessment: Called the most actionable insight by pair_3 debate. Reveals that equity and credit channels dominate Fed loosening, making conditions fragile to market reversals. Asymmetric cross-central-bank implication: ECB more FX-sensitive because European equity/credit provide less loosening.
limitations: Precise magnitude depends on FCI construction methodology
Channel decomposition may not be stable over time
Equity/credit channels are reflexive and could reverse rapidly
Cross-provider comparison of FCI methodologies not resolved

purpose: Applying probability theory to deflate narratives that require multiple independent institutional prerequisites to materialize simultaneously.
components: Identification of required institutional prerequisites for BRICS dedollarization
Assessment of individual prerequisite probabilities
Multiplication for joint probability (<0.1%)
Comparison against narrative-implied probability
current_assessment: The single most effective analytical deflation of the dedollarization narrative across all agents. Survived all four debates. Applicable beyond BRICS to any multi-prerequisite narrative.
limitations: Assumes independence of prerequisites (some may be correlated)
Point probability estimates for individual prerequisites are themselves uncertain
Framework cannot capture gradual institutional evolution vs discrete achievement
